methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in AI & Software (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 38 same-sector peers exist in AI & Software, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

data.narrative:
HUMAIN is a sovereign-stage AI & Software company backed by PIF, with CEO Tareq Amin, having raised $40B+ in capitalized commitments. The AI & Software sector contains 38 peers across public and private markets, though the addressable comp universe for valuation is constrained: one public comparable (PLTR) and eight private peers including Palantir, OpenAI, xAI, Anthropic, and Databricks.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. Despite 38 same-sector peers existing, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier datapoints yielded zero matches across all three narrow criteria. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given HUMAIN's distinct scale and stage within AI infrastructure. The sparse, heterogeneous comp set—spanning generative AI (OpenAI, xAI, Anthropic), data infrastructure (Databricks), and defense-tech (Palantir)—lacks sufficient cohesion to support median-based triangulation.

Without triangulated peer multiples, this analysis cannot establish a defensible valuation range from comparable methods alone. Practitioners should consider alternative approaches: DCF sensitivity analysis anchored to HUMAIN's stated capital base and sector growth assumptions, or sovereign-wealth-backed precedent analysis. The core uncertainty is not sector fundamentals but the absence of sufficiently similar stage/scale/business-model peers. Additional transparency on revenue, deployment timeline, and capital deployment schedule would enable more rigorous benchmarking against the discrete peer set identified.
